nl_query: For each course in the current academic year, show the number of students registered, number of scripts attempted, number absent, number barred, and the pass percentage based on published results with final marks at least 40 percent of the paper total.
failed_sql: SELECT au.code AS course_code, au.name AS course_name, COUNT(DISTINCT sc.id) AS registered_students, COUNT(DISTINCT a.id) AS scripts_attempted, COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN a.lifecycle_status = 'ABSENT' THEN a.id END) AS absent_students, COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN a.lifecycle_status = 'BARRED' THEN a.id END) AS barred_students, ROUND(100.0 * COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN r.final_marks >= 0.4 * qp.total_marks THEN a.id END) / NULLIF(COUNT(DISTINCT a.id), 0), 2) AS pass_percentage FROM academic_unit au JOIN student_cache sc ON au.id = sc.course_id JOIN answer_script a ON a.student_id = sc.id JOIN exam_schedule_cache esc ON a.exam_id = esc.id JOIN question_paper qp ON qp.id = esc.qp_id JOIN result r ON r.script_id = a.id WHERE r.published_at IS NOT NULL AND esc.academic_year = '2025-2026' GROUP BY au.code, au.name ORDER BY pass_percentage ASC;
error: Validation failed (schema): Column 'sc.course_id' does not exist. The Postgres planner suggests 'qp.course_id' — use that, or verify the column belongs to the qualifying table in your FROM/JOIN clauses.
